overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on topics related to computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. LinkedIn accounts for the largest follower base, while Bluesky demonstrates the most recent activity. Overall engagement seems highest on LinkedIn, likely due to its professional networking focus.

contentThemes.description: Focus on research methodologies, data analysis, and findings in the field.
Discussion and analysis of disinformation campaigns and coordinated activity on social media platforms.
Announcements for research grants, postdoctoral positions, and PhD opportunities.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach.
Encourage more interactive discussions by posing questions and soliciting feedback.
Use platform-specific hashtags and keywords to improve discoverability.
Share visual content (images, videos) to increase engagement.
Experiment with different posting times to identify optimal engagement windows.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good level of engagement with research announcements and conference presentations, suggesting an active academic community. Further focus on emerging research trends and community building might be beneficial.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves as a channel for sharing project updates and participating in broader discussions. Increased interaction with other Mastodon users in the field could boost engagement.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is effective for announcing research grants and job opportunities, leveraging its professional network. Continue to share professional achievements and career-related content.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
